lenses.guidance: Esau's full genealogy (vv 1-43) parallels Cain (Gen 4:17-24) and Ishmael (Gen 25:12-18) — the non-elect brother always receives a complete account before the canonical narrative narrows. Compare Edom's later prominence in the prophets.

Edom's kings 'before any king reigned over the Israelites' (v 31) shows Abraham's promise of kings (17:6) advancing first on the rejected line — God's redemptive plan unfolds across both branches before settling on Judah.
